primary_sources.relevance: Defines minimal Merkle consistency proof generation and verification and bounds proof length by ceil(log2(n)) + 1.

finding: A prior checkpoint can be verified as an append-only prefix of a later checkpoint using only logarithmically many subtree hashes, without disclosing or reprocessing the full earlier event history.
design_implication: Peer-trigger checkpoint exchange can carry compact RFC 9162-style consistency paths. Receivers can reject mutation, truncation, incomplete paths, malformed hashes, and roots that are not consistent with the advertised prior checkpoint.

verification.source_review: Committed module was re-fetched at commit dac80a61f03ea0d25efff7167948536955d7f3b1 and matched the intended RFC 9162 recursion and verifier state machine.
verification.isolated_algorithm_test: 136 prefix transitions passed for all 0 < first < second <= 17 using byte-equivalent algorithm logic; proof-size bounds were asserted.
verification.committed_test_status: Committed adversarial suite exists but no provider CI execution was observed in this run.
covered_cases: all prefix transitions through a 17-leaf non-power-of-two tree
old checkpoint not matching the new-history prefix

claim_state.observed: R-007 used full-prefix verification and explicitly handed off compact consistency proofs.
claim_state.implemented: R-008 adds compact proof generation, generic verification, and peer-trigger checkpoint wrappers.
claim_state.inferred: Compact proofs reduce verification transfer and reprocessing from linear history size to logarithmic proof size.
claim_state.proposed: Attach these paths to independently exchanged or signed checkpoints.
claim_state.unresolved: Checkpoint authenticity, witness independence, equivocation gossip, and provider CI execution remain unproven.

trust_limits: A valid consistency proof proves append-only relation between two supplied roots, not that either root is authentic.
A malicious source can present different internally consistent forks to isolated observers without witness exchange.
Merkle integrity does not prove that event payload claims are truthful.
SHA-256 and domain separation are fixed by this implementation; algorithm agility is not implemented.
falsification_route: Reject when any proof node is not a 32-byte hexadecimal hash.
Reject when first and second sizes do not satisfy 0 < first < second.
Reject when reconstruction does not exactly reproduce both advertised roots.
Reject when the proof has excess or insufficient nodes.
Reject peer-specific generation when the old checkpoint root is not the root of the advertised new-history prefix.
Do not accept authenticity or non-equivocation claims without signatures or independent witness comparison.
